c74object.name: dk.spectralonset~
c74object.description: The dk.spectralonset~ object takes audio input and outputs a bang and a trigger when spectral difference is detected. The difference can be computed across different time frames and for different parameters.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithm have a look at this article:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/onsetslice

inlet.description: Audio input to analyze for novelty.

outlet.description: bang output when an onset is detected.
Trigger output when an onset is detected.
Gate output when an onset is detected.

attribute.name: input
attribute.digest: Sets the input mode (air microphone, SP Sensor, contact/trigger)
attribute.description:
Sets the input mode allowing you to use an air microphone, a Sensory Percussion sensor, or a normal drum trigger/contact mic.
The available options are:
0 = unfiltered audio directly into onset detection
1 = 5k bump tuned for use with a Sensory Percussion sensor
2 = 2k bandpass tuned for use with a contact mic or drum trigger

attribute.name: lockout
attribute.digest: Amount of time (in ms) before it can get triggered again
attribute.description: Amount of time (in ms) before an onset can get triggered again.

attribute.name: metric
attribute.digest: The metric on which spectral difference is computed
attribute.description:
The metric used to derive a difference curve between spectral frames. It can be any of the following:
0 = '0'
Energy thresholds on (sum of squares of magnitudes / nBins) (like Onsets power)
1 = '1'
HFC thresholds on (sum of (squared magnitudes * binNum) / nBins)
2 = '2'
SpectralFlux thresholds on (difference in magnitude between consecutive frames, half rectified)
3 = '3'
MKL thresholds on (sum of log of magnitude ratio per bin) (or equivalently, sum of difference of the log magnitude per bin) (like Onsets mkl)
4 = '4'
IS Itakura-Saito divergence (see literature)
5 = '5'
Cosine thresholds on (cosine distance between comparison frames)
6 = '6'
PhaseDev takes the past 2 frames, projects to the current, as anticipated if it was a steady state, then compute the sum of the differences, on which it thresholds (like Onsets phase)
7 = '7'
WPhaseDev same as PhaseDev, but weighted by the magnitude in order to remove chaos noise floor (like Onsets wphase)
8 = '8'
ComplexDev same as PhaseDev, but in the complex domain - the anticipated amp is considered steady, and the phase is projected, then a complex subtraction is done with the actual present frame. The sum of magnitudes is used to threshold (like Onsets complex)
9 = '9'
RComplexDev same as above, but rectified (like Onsets rcomplex)

attribute.name: sensitivity
attribute.digest: The nervousness of the algorithm thresholds (from 0 to 100%)
attribute.description: The nervousness of the algorithm thresholds (from 0 to 100%). This is essentially a 'macro' parameter for the individual on/off thresholds.

attribute.name: smoothing
attribute.digest: The size of the smoothing filter applied to the difference curve
attribute.description: The size of the smoothing filter applied to the difference curve.

attribute.name: window
attribute.digest: Window of time to look for spectral difference in (for HFC, SpectralFlux, MKL, Cosine only)
attribute.description: Window of time to look for spectral difference (for HFC, SpectralFlux, MKL, Cosine only). (in ms)
